yearly.data_source: 1991–2020 climate normals: NOAA GSOD station observations for temperature/dew point when coverage passes audit; NASA POWER/MERRA-2 daily reanalysis for cloud, precipitation, wind, solar energy and gap-free city coverage
yearly.method_note: Daily curves are 15-day circular-smoothed 1991-2020 day-of-year normals. Cloud categories classify each historical day's mean sky cover into five 20-percentage-point bands before calculating day-of-year frequencies. Rainfall is a centered rolling 31-day climatological accumulation. The hourly month-by-hour heatmap remains a separately labelled OneBuilding TMY reference.
yearly.overview: 热带/亚热带气候，全年气温较高。 湿润多雨。

methodology.source: OneBuilding EPW/TMYx-CSWD typical meteorological year files; Page structure and scoring thresholds are generated from local climate-processing rules, not copied raw data.
methodology.temperature: Monthly high/low use the average of daily highs/lows, matching the climate-page convention better than one-month extremes.

tourism_score.formula: 0.50 * apparent-temperature score + 0.25 * cloud score + 0.25 * precipitation score, averaged across daytime hours by month
tourism_score.cloud_thresholds: Clear=10, mostly clear≈9, overcast=1; linear interpolation.
tourism_score.precip_thresholds: No precipitation=10, trace≈9, >=1 mm/hour=0; linear interpolation.

prefecture_city_catalog.station_source: OneBuilding Asia index pages for China / Hong Kong / Macau

climate_normals.cloud_categories: Daily mean sky cover classified as clear 0-<20%, mostly clear 20-<40%, partly cloudy 40-<60%, mostly cloudy 60-<80%, and overcast 80-100%; category frequencies use a 15-day centered circular window across 1991-2020.
climate_normals.rainfall: 31-day centered rolling accumulation of mean daily precipitation
climate_normals.hourly_heatmap_boundary: OneBuilding TMY hourly structure retained as a separate reference; it is not a 30-year climatology.
